title: Variables 4
description: Variables in Rust are immutable by default. To change a variable's value after assignment, you must declare it as mutable.
instructions: Make the code compile by making the variable mutable.
hints: By default, variables in Rust are immutable - you cannot change their value.
To make a variable mutable, use the `mut` keyword.
explanation: Rust variables are immutable by default. Adding `mut` to the declaration allows reassignment. This design choice helps prevent bugs by making mutability an explicit, conscious decision rather than the default behavior.
